narrative.headline: 2022 Tesla Model 3: driver-assist complaints dominate
narrative.summary: The dominant pattern in owner complaints centers on the forward collision avoidance system triggering unexpectedly during normal highway and rural driving, often with no apparent hazard present. A closely related set of reports describes sudden, unintended braking events — sometimes severe — occurring while Autopilot or Traffic-Aware Cruise Control is engaged on clear roads. Electrical system issues and concerns about the speed control system round out the complaint profile, with some incidents involving actual crashes or fires.

issues.title: Forward Collision Avoidance
issues.blurb: Owners report the forward collision avoidance system activating multiple times in a single drive on open highway and rural roads, with no apparent obstacle or hazard to justify the intervention. · 14 crash · 11 injured

issues.title: Vehicle Speed Control
issues.blurb: Owners report that the Traffic-Aware Cruise Control system behaves unpredictably and unsafely, with some describing it as unusable and noting the absence of a conventional cruise control alternative. · 12 crash · 11 injured

issues.title: Service Brakes
issues.blurb: Owners report the vehicle suddenly and forcefully applying the brakes while on Autopilot on clear roads, with no vehicles or obstructions ahead, including incidents triggered by the presence of a school bus that never entered the travel lane. · 14 crash · 12 injured

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: Owners report abrupt, unexplained braking events during Autopilot use that they associate with electrical system behavior, with some incidents also involving fires or crashes. · 3 fire · 6 crash · 6 injured

issues.title: Lane Departure
issues.blurb: The smallest area at 43 reports (6%), but linked to 8 crashes and 1 injury. A less common but recurring complaint.
